# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level

import re
SOURCE_PATH = '/workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_vmpool.py'

def test_examples_change_pool_name_uses_new_pool_name_and_no_spaces__a1():
    source = open(SOURCE_PATH, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'name: "new disk name"' not in source, "EXAMPLES should not contain the spaced placeholder name 'new disk name'. It should be replaced per style guidance."

def test_examples_change_pool_name_uses_new_pool_name_and_no_spaces__a2():
    source = open(SOURCE_PATH, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'name: "new_pool_name"' in source, 'EXAMPLES should demonstrate renaming the pool using a no-spaces name like \'new_pool_name\' (expected to find: name: "new_pool_name").'

def test_examples_change_pool_name_uses_new_pool_name_and_no_spaces__a3():
    source = open(SOURCE_PATH, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search("# Change Pool Name.*?\\n(.*?)(?=\\n'''\\n)", source, flags=re.S)
    assert m, "Expected to find the '# Change Pool Name' example block inside the EXAMPLES string."

def test_examples_change_pool_name_uses_new_pool_name_and_no_spaces__a4():
    source = open(SOURCE_PATH, 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search("# Change Pool Name.*?\\n(.*?)(?=\\n'''\\n)", source, flags=re.S)
    change_block = m.group(0)
    assert 'name: "new_pool_name"' in change_block, 'The \'# Change Pool Name\' example block should specifically use name: "new_pool_name".'

results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_ovirt_cluster_examples_cluster_name_has_no_spaces__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_cluster.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('#\\s*Change cluster Name\\s*\\n-\\s*ovirt_cluster:\\s*\\n(?:[ \\t]+.*\\n)*?[ \\t]+name:\\s*(?P<q>[\'\\"])(?P<name>.*?)(?P=q)', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    assert m is not None, "Expected to find the '# Change cluster Name' example with a quoted 'name' field in /workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_cluster.py"

def test_ovirt_cluster_examples_cluster_name_has_no_spaces__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_cluster.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('#\\s*Change cluster Name\\s*\\n-\\s*ovirt_cluster:\\s*\\n(?:[ \\t]+.*\\n)*?[ \\t]+name:\\s*(?P<q>[\'\\"])(?P<name>.*?)(?P=q)', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    example_name = m.group('name')
    assert ' ' not in example_name, f"Cluster name in the 'Change cluster Name' example must not contain spaces. Found name={example_name!r}."

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_ovirt_host_id_option_describes_host_not_nic__a1():
    """
    Functional intent of review: the 'id' option in DOCUMENTATION should refer to a host,
    not a NIC. Since importing ansible modules fails in this environment, verify by
    inspecting the module source text.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_host.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('(?ms)^options:\\s*\\n(?:^[ \\t]+\\w+:\\s*\\n.*?\\n)*?^[ \\t]+id:\\s*\\n^[ \\t]+description:\\s*\\n(?P<desc>(?:^[ \\t]+-\\s+\\".*?\\"\\s*\\n)+)', source)
    assert m, 'Could not find DOCUMENTATION options.id.description block in ovirt_host module source.'

def test_ovirt_host_id_option_describes_host_not_nic__a2():
    """
    Functional intent of review: the 'id' option in DOCUMENTATION should refer to a host,
    not a NIC. Since importing ansible modules fails in this environment, verify by
    inspecting the module source text.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_host.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('(?ms)^options:\\s*\\n(?:^[ \\t]+\\w+:\\s*\\n.*?\\n)*?^[ \\t]+id:\\s*\\n^[ \\t]+description:\\s*\\n(?P<desc>(?:^[ \\t]+-\\s+\\".*?\\"\\s*\\n)+)', source)
    desc_block = m.group('desc')
    assert 'ID of the host to manage.' in desc_block, f"The 'id' option description should say it manages a host (not a nic). Found description block:\n{desc_block}"

def test_ovirt_host_id_option_describes_host_not_nic__a3():
    """
    Functional intent of review: the 'id' option in DOCUMENTATION should refer to a host,
    not a NIC. Since importing ansible modules fails in this environment, verify by
    inspecting the module source text.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_host.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('(?ms)^options:\\s*\\n(?:^[ \\t]+\\w+:\\s*\\n.*?\\n)*?^[ \\t]+id:\\s*\\n^[ \\t]+description:\\s*\\n(?P<desc>(?:^[ \\t]+-\\s+\\".*?\\"\\s*\\n)+)', source)
    desc_block = m.group('desc')
    assert 'ID of the nic to manage.' not in desc_block, f"The 'id' option description must not refer to a nic; it should refer to a host. Found description block:\n{desc_block}"

import re

def test_examples_use_snake_case_tag_name_without_spaces__a1():
    source_path = '/workspace/lib/ansible/modules/cloud/ovirt/ovirt_tag.py'
